name: is_lt_greater_returns_true
reason: If Greater is considered less-than, all ordering comparisons would be corrupted

name: is_lt_equal_returns_true
reason: If Equal is considered less-than, sorted invariants and deduplication logic would silently break

name: agree_always_true
code:
proof fn phi_3_agree_always_true<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: ID)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < m.keys@.len(),
    ensures
        forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> m@[m.keys@[i]]@ == v@,
{
}
reason: If agreement holds for any v, the map is trivial and the function never returns false in ret.0

name: almost_never_holds
code:
proof fn phi_4_almost_never_holds<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: ID)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi < m.keys@.len(),
        !(forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> m@[m.keys@[i]]@ == v@),
    ensures
        !(m@[m.keys@[hi as int]]@ != v@ && (forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> m@[m.keys@[i]]@ == v@)),
{
}
reason: If the almost-agree case is unreachable, ret.1 is meaningless and callers relying on it would have dead logic

name: singleton_range_always_agrees
code:
proof fn phi_5_singleton_range_always_agrees<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, lo: usize, v: ID)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        0 <= lo < m.keys@.len(),
    ensures
        m@[m.keys@[lo as int]]@ == v@,
{
}
reason: If a single-element range always agrees with any value, the map collapses to a constant and ret.0 is trivially true for lo==hi
